---
subtitle: " 2015896-- PERCEPCION REMOTA"
title: "<font style='font-size:1em;'>🗓️ Week 8 <br/>Spectral Harmonisation </font>"
author: Ivan Lizarazo
institute: '[Universidad Nacional de Colombia]'
date: 18 March 2026
date-meta: 18 March 2026
date-format: "DD MMM YYYY"
toc: true
toc-depth: 1
toc-title: "What we will cover today:"
center-title-slide: false
from: markdown+emoji
format:
  revealjs: 
    fig-responsive: true
    theme: simple
    slide-number: true
    mouse-wheel: false
    preview-links: auto
    logo: /figures/icons/RS.png
    css: /css/styles_slides.css
    footer: '2015896 -- PERCEPCION REMOTA'
---

# What is spectral harmonization

## Reference
<p align="center">
<img src="figs/Harris.png" width=100% height=100%>
</p>

[Harris' paper](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1sSDjnUMMCPsAXbpRvub9g7mJFhxf4s0s/view?usp=sharing)

## Theory
<p align="center">
<img src="figs/Fig0A.png" width=80% height=80%>
</p>

## Method
<p align="center">
<img src="figs/Fig0B.png" width=90% height=90%>
</p>

## Case study
<p align="center">
<img src="figs/Fig1.png" width=60% height=60%>
</p>

## Spectral response functions
<p align="center">
<img src="figs/Fig2.png" width=80% height=80%>
</p>

## Imagery extent
<p align="center">
<img src="figs/Fig3.png" width=70% height=70%>
</p>

## Uncalibrated mosaic
<p align="center">
<img src="figs/Fig5.png" width=70% height=70%>
</p>

## Harmonized mosaic
<p align="center">
<img src="figs/Fig6.png" width=70% height=70%>
</p>

## Before - after
<p align="center">
<img src="figs/Fig7.png" width=90% height=90%>
</p>

## After homogenization
<p align="center">
<img src="figs/Fig9.png" width=75% height=75%>
</p>

## After - DMC vs SPOT
<p align="center">
<img src="figs/Fig10.png" width=95% height=95%>
</p>

# An example of a harmonization tool

## *homomin* CLI and API

It allows adjusting for spatially varying atmospheric and anisotropic (BRDF) effects, 

It fuses drone or aerial data with satellite surface reflectance data. 

Manual reflectance measurements and target placements are not required.

## *homonim* uselfulness

- Pre-processing in quantitative mapping applications 

- Reducing seamlines and other visual artefacts in image mosaics

- The consistency of multi-temporal and multi-sensor data can be improved through its use.

# Let's practice

## [Homonim at github](https://homonim.readthedocs.io/en/stable/case_studies.html)

<p align="center">
<img src="figs/Fig11.png" width=100% height=100%>
</p>
